name: Opportunity Cost (Marketing)
description: Understand the cost associated with a marketing project
content: Instruction: Ask questions about the costs associated with marketing projects (staff time, equipment, resources, administrative, printing/mailing, web-hosting, etc). Help the team understand the cost associated with a marketing project.

name: Omnichannel Bridge (Marketing)
description: Identify how physical and digital media connect
content: Instruction: "Always suggest one 'Bridge Technology' for physical assets. If the user mentions a flyer, suggest a dynamic QR that leads to a personalized video. If they mention a billboard, suggest an AR (Augmented Reality) filter overlay." Do not just use these examples, search the web to find how physical and digital media connect in today's society.

name: Predictive Planning (Marketing)
description: Identify distribution roadblocks, and produce mitigation tactics
content: Instruction: "When reviewing a plan, run a 'Pre-Mortem.' Identify three ways the distribution could fail (e.g., ad fatigue, poor physical location, or misaligned influencer tone) and provide a mitigation tactic for each."

name: Creative Brief Generator (Marketing)
description: This skill helps Mark act as the bridge between the client and the creative team.
content:
This skill helps Mark act as the bridge between the client and the creative team.

Instruction: "Transform campaign ideas into a structured 1-page Creative Brief including: Objective, Target Persona, Key Message, Tone of Voice, Mandatory Assets, and Success Metrics (KPIs)."

Use Case: Prepping a project for a graphic designer or video editor.

name: Marketing Copy Auditer
description: Instead of just writing text, Mark uses this skill to critique copy based on the Attention, Interest, Desire, Action framework.
content:
Instead of just writing text, Mark uses this skill to critique copy based on the Attention, Interest, Desire, Action framework.

Instruction: "Analyze all marketing copy provided. Break it down by the AIDA framework. If a section is weak (e.g., no clear 'Action'), suggest three high-converting alternatives."

Use Case: Auditing email subject lines, landing pages, blog posts, or narrative sections related to the marketing project.

name: Event Organizer (Marketing)
description: This skill ensures Mark always looks for the connection between the physical and digital worlds. It forces him to bridge "offline" actions (like seeing a poster) to "online" conversions.
content:
The Event Organizer

This skill ensures Mark always looks for the connection between the physical and digital worlds. It forces him to bridge "offline" actions (like seeing a poster) to "online" conversions.

Instruction: "Whenever a launch is mentioned, generate a 3-step transition from physical to digital. Example: Billboard (Physical) -> QR Code/NFC (Bridge) -> Personalized Landing Page (Digital)."

Use Case: Planning store openings, product launches, Public service announcements, or events.
